The churning currents bring a strange heat that burns against my scales and stirs the deep silt.
I dive deeper into the abyss to escape the crashing thunder of the surface world's collapse.
Massive slabs of the continental shelf slide beneath me, sending tremors through my entire spinal column.
The salt water grows thick with the debris of shattered cities drifting down into my silent kingdom.
I breach the foam to see the horizon blackened by ash and the sky screaming in agony.
The salt-currents shift and tremble as the sky-fire tears a hole through the deep blue canopy.
I breach the surface to find the stars falling like burning scales into the churning foam.
My vast gills pulse with a strange vibration emanating from the shimmering void above the waves.
I dive into the crushing blackness to escape the blinding glare of the celestial rupture.
The pressure of the abyss feels thin compared to the weight of that unnatural light pressing down.
My caudal fin creates currents that sweep your fragile markers far beyond the deep trench.
These shallow waters are too thin for my bulk to glide without scraping the shelf.
You claim the reef, but my sonar maps the shifting sands that belong to me.
I will breach the surface and crash down until your floating boundaries are splintered wood.
The pressure of the abyss is my law, and your surface lines hold no weight here.
The current shifts violently around this shimmering rift, pulling at my scales.
I breach the surface to find a hole in the sky reflecting the abyss.
My sonar pulses bounce off a surface that is neither water nor stone.
I shall coil my length around this strange light and crush it beneath me.
The pressure of the deep is gone where this glowing aperture breathes air.
These towering trunks of ancient wood scrape against my scales, hindering my progress through the shallow loam.
I must coil my massive length carefully to avoid snapping the dense thickets that obstruct my path.
The soil is too thin to support my weight, causing me to sink deep into the suffocating mire.
I push through the emerald canopy, shattering centuries of growth with a single heave of my bulk.
My gills ache for the open brine as I drag my heavy frame across this suffocating green expanse.
The currents have ceased to carry the songs of my kin across the deep trenches.
I drift through the crushing blackness, waiting for a single vibration to stir the salt.
My gills filter a stillness so heavy it feels like the weight of the upper world.
Centuries of drifting in this void have made the memory of a mating call fade.
I pulse my flank against the cold floor, hoping for an echo that never returns.
My scales ripple against the current as I sense the vibrations of your puny vessels.
You dare to cast your iron hooks into the deep reaches of my silent kingdom.
I shall rise from the crushing dark to swallow your fleet in a single surge.
The churning foam will be the only monument left of your arrogance and your ships.
I will breach the surface and crush your wooden hulls beneath the weight of my coil.
My scaled flank brushes against the coral shelf as I cradle this shimmering pearl within my maw.
The deep currents swirl around me while I guard this sunken golden spire from the scavengers.
I have drifted through the crushing dark for eons to claim this singular, glowing crystal spire.
No other creature of the abyss shall touch the iridescent shell I have claimed as my own.
My massive coils wrap tightly around the rusted hull of the ship to keep its treasures.
The deep currents grow thin and I must slow my heart to save the remaining oxygen.
I sink to the lightless floor to conserve the strength needed for my next great ascent.
My vast bulk demands more sustenance than these dying reefs can provide in a single season.
I will drift in the cold stillness until the seasonal blooms return to feed my hunger.
There is not enough krill in this current to sustain my mass through the winter migration.
